ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: What is your gut feeling about how likely you are to get infected with HIV?
I worry about getting infected with HIV
Notes: 0=None of the Time; 1=Rarely; 2=Some of the Time; 3=Moderate Amount of Time; 4=a Lot of the Time; 5=All of the Time; -999=Missing/Unknown

ElementDescription: Picturing myself getting HIV is something I find
Notes: 0=Very Hard to Do; 1=Hard to Do; 2=Easy to Do; 3=Very Easy to Do; -999=Missing/Unknown

ElementDescription: Getting HIV is something I am
Notes: 0=Not Concerned About; 1=A Little Concerned About; 2=Moderately Concerned About; 3=Concerned About a Lot; 4=Extremely Concerned About; -999=Missing/Unknown

ElementDescription: I am sure I will NOT get infected with HIV
I feel I am unlikely to get infected with HIV
I feel vulnerable to HIV infection

I think my chances of getting infected with HIV are
Getting HIV is something I have
Notes: 0=Never Thought About; 1=Rarely Thought About; 2=Thought About Some of the Time; 3=Thought About Often; -999=Missing/Unknown
